user_query:
I want to quantify code ownership risk after our latest management reshuffle using only the local files in this workspace. Please do the following and produce two artifacts: output/report.json and output/summary.csv.

Inputs to use:
- input/teams.yaml: canonical team map with status and managers.
- input/org_changes.csv: event log of team changes.
- repos/*/CODEOWNERS: ownership by path pattern per repo (normalize owners by stripping leading '@').
- repos/*/(pyproject.toml or package.json): maintainer names for each repo.

Requirements:
1) For each repository under repos/, parse CODEOWNERS entries into a list of {pattern, owner}. Look up each owner (team slug) in input/teams.yaml:
   - owner_status is one of: active, merged_into, dissolved, or unknown (if not found in teams.yaml).
   - If status is merged_into, include merged_into target team (from teams.yaml).
   - Mark a pattern as impacted if owner_status is not "active" (i.e., merged_into, dissolved, or unknown).
2) Determine dominant_owner per repo as the owner assigned to the root pattern "/". If missing, dominant_owner = null and treat maintainer alignment as "mismatch".
3) Extract maintainer names from pyproject.toml (project.maintainers array of name fields) or package.json (maintainers array of name fields). Compute maintainer_alignment per repo:
   - "aligned" if at least one maintainer name equals the manager_after of the dominant_owner team from teams.yaml;
   - otherwise "mismatch". If dominant_owner is null or unknown, treat as "mismatch".
4) Cross-validation against input/org_changes.csv:
   - For every team appearing in CODEOWNERS with teams.yaml status in {merged_into, dissolved}, assert a matching row exists in org_changes.csv with change_type merged or dissolved for that team; otherwise record an inconsistency.
   - For every row in org_changes.csv with change_type in {merged, dissolved}, assert the same team in teams.yaml does not have status active; otherwise record an inconsistency.
   - Collect any CODEOWNERS owners not present in teams.yaml as unknown_owners.
5) Write output/report.json with this structure:
   {
     "repos": [
       {
         "name": "<repo name>",
         "dominant_owner": "<team or null>",
         "maintainer_names": ["..."],
         "maintainer_alignment": "aligned|mismatch",
         "patterns": [
           {"pattern": "...", "owner": "...", "owner_status": "active|merged_into|dissolved|unknown", "merged_into": "<team if applicable or null>", "canonical_name": "<from teams.yaml if known>", "impacted": true|false}
         ],
         "counts": {"total_patterns": N, "impacted_patterns": M, "percent_impacted": <0-100 float or int>}
       }
     ],
     "validation": {
       "unknown_owners": ["..."],
       "missing_root_owner": ["<repo names missing '/'>"],
       "org_mismatches": ["<human-readable lines describing any of the two cross-validation failures above>"]
     }
   }
6) Write output/summary.csv with columns: repo,total_patterns,impacted_patterns,percent_impacted,maintainer_alignment. Values must be consistent with report.json.

Do not fetch anything from the Internet. Derive all results purely from the provided files. Ensure paths in the outputs are exactly output/report.json and output/summary.csv. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
